I found the tables practically unhelpful. Further, his sex-table
is alright; but the other table begins its count with Krittika.
Why should we begin with this constellation? Can't we begin
either with Aswini or with Uttarabhadra?

Directions: Some Attempted Equations 115
In the end let me point out clearly that at times where all
f other methods have failed, there we can get a clue from
the dasa system. From the nature of the results embodied
in the dasas, we can adjust the time of birth to a very great
extent.

9
Progression by Averages
Primary and Secondary directions are incapable of rectifying
the time of birth .. If a predicted event on the basis of these
directions does not happen, we can say either the given time is
incorrect or these directions are of no value. The solar-return
chart may at times enable us to correct the time within ten or
fifteen minutes. This is not enough. We want exactness.
An easier method has been proposed by some. It is a kind
